data.title: Aptera Motors Raises $6.34 Million Via Warrant Exercises, Issues 4.75M New Warrants at $3.50
data.description:
Aptera Motors entered into a Warrant Inducement Agreement with holders of its existing warrants to generate immediate cash and enhance liquidity. Holders agreed to cash exercise warrants for up to 3,167,500 shares at $2.00, providing gross proceeds of about $6.34 million. In return, Aptera will issue 4,751,250 new warrants exercisable immediately at $3.50 per share, expiring five years from issuance, and will file a resale registration following its 10-K. The company also agreed to a 30-day issuance lock-up after the resale registration becomes effective, subject to customary exceptions.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Warrant inducement agreement and issuance of five-year common stock warrants
  • Counterparty: Holders of existing warrants
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 12 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years
  • Reason: Raise cash and strengthen liquidity

Original SEC Filing: Aptera Motors Corp [ SEV ] - 8-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: Retail Investors Hit the Brakes Amid Uncertainty, JPMorgan Says — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Everyday traders are showing signs of weakness, interrupting a buy, buy, buy trend for the first time this year.

Monday marked the largest net-selling day for single stocks in a month among retail investors, according to a report by JPMorgan.

Here's what retail trading activity for the week through Wednesday showed:

  • Individual investors' overall purchases slowed by around 30%. They put 22% less into ETFs and also cut back on single-stock purchases.
  • Retail investors sold energy-sector stocks such as ExxonMobil, which has exposure to the Middle East, the most. They also shifted money out of the , which is weighted toward large-cap companies with global footprints, and bought into the , an ETF that tracks WTI futures.
  • In spite of AI-related market concerns, mom-and-pop investors continued to favor the artificial-intelligence trade, including stocks positioned to benefit from data-center buildout.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Mexico's Cemex to Sell Some Assets in Colombia
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

MEXICO CITY--Mexican building materials company Cemex said it is selling part of its assets in Colombia for around $555 million as it focuses on operations in core markets, while maintaining a presence in the South American country.

Cemex has reached an agreement with the Holcim Group to sell a cement plant, a grinding mill, and ready-mix concrete, aggregates, mortar and admixture plants for $485 million, with the sale expected to close at the end of the year.

Cemex of Monterrey, Mexico said it is also negotiating with other third parties to sell its remaining assets in the same geographic area for an estimated $70 million.

Cemex will maintain two cement plants in Colombia with capacity of 1.6 million tons a year, a grinding mill, and ready-mix concrete plants and aggregates quarries.

The sales are part of Cemex's streamlining of its operations with focus on the U.S., Europe and Mexico, chief executive Jaime Muguiro said. "We began our portfolio rebalancing effort in 2018 and have accomplished most of what we have set out to do."

Holcim said the operations it is acquiring from Cemex represent projected 2026 sales of about $360 million and will complement its existing operations in Colombia.

"This acquisition will strengthen Holcim's presence in Colombia and accelerate growth in the attractive region of Latin America," Holcim chief executive Miljan Gutovic said.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: Theriva 10-K: Net loss $23.74M, EPS $(2.08); Operating loss $24.05M
data.description:
Theriva reported a net loss of $23.74 million for the fiscal year, driven by operating losses of $24.05 million and resulting in basic and diluted net loss per share of $(2.08) on 12.14 million weighted average shares; cash and cash equivalents were $13.10 million with management projecting a runway into Q1 2027, subject to risks. The operating loss reflects total operating costs and expenses of $24.05 million, including general and administrative and research and development spending. Management highlighted strategic shifts and financing actions intended to support its oncology-focused programs and near-term liquidity.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating Income (Loss): $(24.05) million (loss from operations per Consolidated Statements of Operations)
  • Net loss: $(23.74) million (net loss per Consolidated Statements of Operations)
  • Net Loss Per Share — Basic and Diluted: $(2.08); weighted average shares 12.14 million
  • Total Operating Costs and Expenses: $24.05 million (includes G&A $15.45 million and R&D $8.60 million)
  • Cash and cash equivalents: $13.10 million (management expects cash runway into Q1 2027, subject to risks)

Business Highlights

  • Strategic focus: Transitioned to oncology following the 2022 acquisition, prioritizing intravenous and intravitreal oncolytic adenovirus VCN-01 and discovery work on VCN-X.
  • Clinical progress: VIRAGE Phase 2b for VCN-01 met primary survival and safety endpoints, prompting contingent consideration events and regulatory planning activities.
  • Workforce and cost actions: Restructured operations in September 2025 and reduced headcount by 32% to reallocate resources toward VCN-01 development and manufacturing scale-up.
  • Out-licensing and partnerships: SYN-020 was exclusively licensed to Rasayana in February 2026 under an agreement that includes upfront payments, development and regulatory milestones, and royalties; SYN-004 is being explored for out-licensing opportunities.
  • Funding and runway management: Management extended operational runway into early 2027 through ATM equity sales, warrant exercises, and license proceeds while pursuing additional partnerships and financing to support development and manufacturing activities.

Original SEC Filing: Theriva Biologics, Inc. [ TOVX ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Candel 2025 10-K: Operating loss $48.27M, Net loss $38.18M, strengthened cash runway
data.description:
Candel reported results for the year ended December 31, 2025, showing an operating loss of $48.266 million and a net loss of $38.182 million; the company recognized $3.915 million of interest income and $0.089 million of grant income in 2025. Operating expenses increased versus 2024 while net loss improved year‑over‑year, and the company strengthened liquidity through equity raises and a $50 million tranche draw to extend runway into 2028. Key clinical and regulatory milestones during the year position Candel for late‑stage development and future regulatory filings.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating expenses: $48.266 million for the year ended Dec 31, 2025 (increase of $14.895 million vs 2024).
  • Loss from operations: $(48.266) million for the year ended Dec 31, 2025 (worsened $14.895 million vs 2024).
  • Net loss: $(38.182) million for the year ended Dec 31, 2025 (improved $16.995 million vs 2024).
  • Interest income: $3.915 million earned in 2025 (increase of $2.829 million vs 2024).
  • Grant income: $0.089 million recognized in 2025 (none in 2024).

Business Highlights

  • Clinical milestone: Phase 3 aglatimagene met the primary disease‑free survival (DFS) endpoint in localized intermediate‑ to high‑risk prostate cancer; positive topline data announced.
  • Pipeline expansion: Ongoing programs include the phase 3 prostate pivotal program, a planned phase 3 program in non‑small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), HSV candidate linoserpaturev in recurrent high‑grade glioma (HGG), and multiple preclinical enLIGHTEN assets.
  • Regulatory momentum: Received FDA Fast Track and RMAT designations for aglatimagene, Fast Track and Orphan Drug designations for linoserpaturev, and an orphan designation in the EU for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC).
  • Operational readiness: Increased R&D and commercial‑readiness spending, initiated manufacturing and biomarker studies, and preparing a Biologics License Application (BLA) submission targeted for 4Q2026.
  • Cash runway & funding: Strengthened liquidity through multiple equity raises and a $50 million tranche draw from Trinity, supporting operations into the first quarter of 2028.

Original SEC Filing: Candel Therapeutics, Inc. [ CADL ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: RWE to ramp up US business with €17bn investment
data.description:
The renewable power generator, Germany’s largest, said it planned to invest a total of €35bn between 2026 and 2031, €17bn of which would be in the US. The intention is to boost installed capacity in America to 22GW from its current level of 13GW.

RWE said there was high power demand growth in the country, driven by data centres and electrification. Artificial intelligence requires vast, power-hungry data centres, and many are built with their own dedicated sources of energy.

RWE also flagged a particular advantage for the technologies it specialises in - wind, solar, battery storage and gas generation - as they have a short time to market. The expansion also mitigated tariff risks, it noted. "The US remains our most important growth market," it said.

Markus Krebber, chief executive, said: "With our €35bn net investment programme through to 2031, we are building new generation capacity to meet the growing demand for electricity in Europe and the United States.

"In the US, we are broadening our portfolio and now focusing on gas in addition to renewables.

"Our investments will pay off: our adjusted earnings per share will grow from €2.48 today to €4.40m by 2031."

As at 1145 GMT, the Frankfurt listed-stock had put on 3%.

The update came as Essen-based RWE posted full-year results showing a fall in adjusted earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation, to €5.1bn from €5.7bn a year previously, driven in part by lower earnings at its offshore wind division. It was, however, at the upper end of guidance.

Adjusted net income fell to €1.8bn from €2.3bn, also at the upper end of guidance.

Looking to the current year, RWE said it expected operating earnings to improve, supported by the commissioning of new wind and solar farms and battery storage facilities.

Adjusted EBITDA was forecast to come in between €5.2bn and €5.8bn, and net income between €1.55bn and €2.05bn.

It also forecast a further rise in earnings and net income in 2027.

data.title: MBX Biosciences 10-K: Operating loss $98.1M, Net loss $87.0M — cash runway into 2029
data.description:
MBX Biosciences reported an operating loss of $98.1 million and a net loss of $87.0 million for the year, reflecting increased investment in research and development and general and administrative costs; interest and other income, net was $11.1 million, supported by higher interest income. The company holds $373.7 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, which management estimates is sufficient to fund operations into 2029. Revenue and EPS were not disclosed in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Operating expenses: $98.1 million, an increase of $29.9 million versus the prior year driven by higher R&D and G&A spending.
  • Loss from operations: $(98.1) million, a $(29.9) million increase versus the prior year.
  • Net loss: $(87.0) million, a $(25.0) million increase versus the prior year.
  • Interest and other income, net: $11.1 million, up $4.8 million due to higher interest income.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities: $373.7 million, estimated to fund operations into 2029.

Business Highlights

  • Pipeline progress: Advanced lead candidates with planned and expected clinical milestones — canvuparatide Phase 3 planned for Q3 2026; imapextide Phase 2a topline expected Q2 2026; MBX 4291 Phase 1 MAD results expected Q4 2026.
  • Clinical outcomes: Positive Phase 2 results for canvuparatide and Phase 1 safety/PK data for canvuparatide and imapextide support potential once‑weekly dosing.
  • Capital and runway: Completed IPO and follow-on offerings during 2024–2026 to fund clinical and operational milestones through the projected runway into 2029.
  • R&D scale-up: Increased R&D investment and headcount to support multiple clinical programs, discovery pipeline activities, and planned candidate nominations in 2026.
  • Collaborations & IP: Exclusive license with IURTC covering core assets (canvuparatide, imapextide, MBX 4291) with milestone and royalty arrangements to support future commercialization.

Original SEC Filing: MBX Biosciences, Inc. [ MBX ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: BTB REIT Announces its Distribution for the Month of March 2026
data.description:
MONTRÉAL, March 12, 2026 /CNW/ - BTB Real Estate Investment Trust ("BTB" or the "REIT") announces today that the monthly cash distribution for the month of March 2026 is $0.025 per unit, representing $0.30 per unit on an annualized basis. The cash distribution will be paid on April 16(th) , 2026, to unitholders of record on March 31(st) , 2026.

About BTB

BTB is a real estate investment trust listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange. BTB invests in industrial, suburban office and necessity-based retail properties across Canada for the benefit of their investors. As of today, BTB owns and manages 72 properties, representing a total leasable area of approximately 6.0 million square feet.

People and their stories are at the heart of our success.

For more detailed information, visit BTB's website at www.btbreit.com.

data.title: Astrana Health reports $3.18B revenue, $0.46 EPS in 10-K
data.description:
Astrana Health reported total revenue of $3,181.8 million for the year, up 56% from 2024 driven largely by the July Prospect acquisition; diluted earnings per share were $0.46, down from $0.90 a year earlier amid higher interest and integration costs. While adjusted EBITDA rose to $205.4 million, margin compression to 6% reflected acquisition-related and higher medical utilization expenses. The company noted operating income of $78.5 million and net income of $24.1 million for the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $3,181.8 million, up $1,147.2 million (56%) versus 2024; Prospect acquisition contributed approximately $616.3 million.
  • Operating income: $78.5 million, down $10.8 million (−12%) versus 2024, impacted by higher medical utilization and integration costs.
  • Net income: $24.1 million, down $25.9 million (−52%) versus 2024; increased interest expense largely driven by acquisition financing.
  • Earnings per share – diluted: $0.46, down from $0.90 in 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $205.4 million, up $35.1 million (21%) versus 2024; Adjusted EBITDA margin 6% (2024: 8%), reflecting acquisition lift offset by higher costs.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Total revenue growth was driven by the July Prospect acquisition and expansion of capitation revenue.
  • Provider and patient scale: Expanded network to over 20,000 contracted physicians and managed roughly 1.6 million patients nationwide, increasing risk-bearing organizations (RBOs) to 28.
  • Segment momentum: Care Partners and Care Enablement businesses grew substantially following the acquisition; Care Delivery scaled visits but experienced start-up costs.
  • Strategic M&A & integration: Completed acquisition of Prospect, which includes a health plan, medical groups, pharmacy and a hospital, enhancing access and value-based care capabilities.
  • Operational positioning and outlook: Investing in technology-enabled population health management and preparing for policy shifts (including OBBBA and Medicare Advantage rate changes) while maintaining a diversified Medicaid footprint to support resilience and future growth.

Original SEC Filing: Astrana Health, Inc. [ ASTH ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Dollar General Posts Higher 4Q Sales, Issues Cautious Forecast — Update
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Dollar General logged higher profit and sales in its fiscal fourth quarter but its guidance suggests a more measured pace of growth ahead.

The discount retailer on Thursday said it expects comparable sales, or those from stores and digital channels open for at least a year, to grow 2.2% to 2.7% this year. The outlook brackets the 2.5% gain that analysts polled by FactSet had expected, but it is below the 3% increase the company notched last year.

Sales are expected to rise 3.7% to 4.2%, slower than the 5.2% gain the company reported last year. Wall Street projects sales to climb 3.9%.

Dollar General also guided for earnings of $7.10 to $7.35 a share, compared with Wall Street views for $7.25 a share.

Shares fell 5.4%, to $137, in premarket trading.

The outlook came as Dollar General posted a profit of $426.3 million for its three months ended Jan. 30, up from $191.2 million a year earlier. On a per-share basis, quarterly earnings of $1.93 topped the $1.66 that analysts polled by FactSet had expected.

Sales climbed 5.9% to $10.91 billion, ahead of the $10.81 billion that Wall Street had modeled.

Comparable sales were up 4.3% from a year ago, also ahead of the 3.5% increase that analysts had forecast. The increase was driven by a 2.6% increase in customer traffic and a 1.7% gain in average transaction amount, both of which were boosted by growth across categories including consumables, seasonal, home products and apparel.

Chief Executive Todd Vasos said the company advanced key initiatives during the quarter, resulting in strong operating margin expansion and better-than-expected per-share earnings growth.

"Looking ahead to 2026, we are excited about our plans to drive continued growth through a variety of initiatives designed to further enhance the customer experience, elevate our brand, drive greater enterprise-wide efficiencies and extend our reach," he added.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Bitcoin Pares Losses as Oil Prices Pull Back From Highs — Market Talk
data.description: Bitcoin trims its losses to trade back above $70,000 as oil prices pull back from their highs that were driven by the widening Middle East conflict. Brent crude earlier rose back above $100 per barrel before dropping back to last trade at $97.93. The rise followed news that two foreign fuel tankers were attacked in Iraqi waters Wednesday. Oil prices remain the driver of macroeconomic sentiment, Marex crypto analyst Louis De Backer says in a note. When crude spikes the market immediately reprices inflation risks and cryptocurrencies are treated as risk-sensitive assets, he says. Bitcoin falls 0.2% to $70,485, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Indonesia biofuel producers expect June end to B50 test as government eyes earlier completion
data.description:
By Bernadette Christina

Indonesia's biofuel producers association APROBI said on Thursday it expected a roadtest for 50% palm-based biodiesel blending, or B50, to be concluded no earlier than the energy ministry's previous target of June or July.

  • The association's reiteration of the timeline comes despite the energy ministry issuing a statement a day earlier about an acceleration of the B50 tests.

  • Deputy Energy Minister Yuliot Tanjung had said the B50 road test was being expedited in case Indonesia struggles with crude oil supply due to conflict in the Middle East. The aim was to reach 50,000 km of testing by the end of March, he said.

  • APROBI, which is involved in the testing, said the 50,000 km target was expected to be achieved in May, followed by checks on the car’s machinery, which typically take a month.

  • “Whether or not this can be immediately implemented, based on our previous experience, it cannot because the energy ministry needs to change some regulations, including on biodiesel allocations,” APROBI secretary general Ernest Gunawan told a press conference.

  • APROBI said it would support the government’s B50 plan but urged it to conduct thorough calculations before implementing higher blending to avoid a food-versus-energy issue, and continuity of the blending mandate in the coming years.

  • Indonesia has biodiesel installed capacity of 22 million kilolitres, which could produce about 17.6 million kilolitres biodiesel per year. This capacity would be sufficient for a B50 mandate in the second half, but insufficient for a full-year implementation.
